highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 3.119.0 advances the engine to Skia m119 and adds LoongArch64, RISC-V64, and Alpine Linux support.
highlights_body: This release adds Direct3D rendering on Windows, iOS Simulator Metal performance gains, tvOS Metal view support, LoongArch64 and RISC-V64 Linux builds, Alpine Linux cross-compilation, and SKColorFilter additions. It also fixes a .NET Framework packages.config regression.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine moves to m119, bringing upstream rendering improvements and bug fixes.
Skia is now compiled with Direct3D backend support on Windows, unlocking hardware-accelerated GPU rendering via D3D12.
Several SKColorFilter factory methods that were present in Skia but missing from the binding have been exposed.
SKCanvas now exposes the SaveLayerRec overload of SaveLayer, enabling fine-grained control over layer compositing parameters.
A regression in 3.116.0 that broke .NET Framework and Mono projects using packages.config has been corrected.
The iOS Simulator was incorrectly bypassing Metal; this fix restores proper GPU-accelerated rendering in the simulator.
SKImage.FromPicture was producing incorrect output; the implementation has been fixed to match the expected behaviour.
An incorrect native call in SKCanvas has been corrected.

bullets.lead: LoongArch64 and RISC-V64 Linux builds added
bullets.detail: Native assets are now produced for LoongArch64 and RISC-V64 Linux targets, expanding the set of supported architectures.
The clang-cross toolchain has been extended to support Alpine Linux, enabling musl-based Linux builds.
SKMetalView is now available on tvOS, bringing hardware-accelerated rendering to the tvOS platform.

bullets.lead: Metal API available for GRBackendRenderTarget on all platforms
bullets.detail: Metal-specific constructors and accessors for GRBackendRenderTarget are now available on all supported Apple platforms including tvOS.

bullets.lead: Windows ARM GL and tvOS targets corrected
bullets.detail: GL rendering is now enabled on Windows ARM, and tvOS deployment targets have been corrected.
libpng has been updated to 1.6.44, freetype2 to 2.13.3+, libexpat to 2.6.4, libjpeg-turbo to 2.1.5.1+, and HarfBuzz to 8.3.1.

contributor_summaries.kasperk81: Added RISC-V64 and LoongArch64 build support and fixed Linux tests
contributor_summaries.Kation: Added Direct3D rendering support on Windows
contributor_summaries.kkwpsv: Fixed an incorrect native call in SKCanvas
contributor_summaries.sungaila: Fixed the packages.config regression for .NET Framework and Mono projects

preview_summaries.v3.119.0-preview.1.2: Preview 1 introduced LoongArch64 and RISC-V64 Linux support, Direct3D on Windows, tvOS Metal view, Alpine Linux cross-compilation, SaveLayerRec, missing SKColorFilter types, and fixes for packages.config, iOS Simulator Metal, and the SKImage.FromPicture implementation.
preview_summaries.v3.118.0-preview.2.3: Preview 2 of 3.118.0 added Metal GRBackendRenderTarget APIs, fixed .NET 9 Blazor rendering, updated the Android NDK to r27c, and resolved the NuGet PackagePath issue.
preview_summaries.v3.118.0-preview.1.2: Preview 1 of 3.118.0 advanced the bundled Skia engine through milestones m117 and m118 and updated HarfBuzz to 8.3.1.

harfbuzz_summary: Ships HarfBuzzSharp 8.3.1.1 with extended platform coverage for LoongArch64, RISC-V64, Alpine Linux, and corrected ARM and Blazor targets.
